{{神社| 名称=曩祖八幡宮| 所在地=福岡県飯塚市宮町2-3| 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|33|38|27|N|130|41|02|E|scale:30000}}| 祭神=応神天皇
仲哀天皇
神功皇后
武内宿禰
天神地祇| 社格=県社・別表神社| 例祭=[[4月15日]]・[[10月15日]]| 神事=[[放生会|放生会祭]]・曩祖太鼓}} '''曩祖八幡宮'''（のうそはちまんぐう）は、[[福岡県]][[飯塚市]]にある[[神社]]である。旧[[社格]]は[[県社]]で、現在は[[神社本庁]]の[[別表神社]]。旧称'''納祖宮'''。 飯塚の[[鎮守神|鎮守]]として崇敬され、また子守りの神としても信仰されている。 ==祭神== [[応神天皇]]・[[仲哀天皇]]・[[神功皇后]]・[[武内宿禰]]および天神地祇を祀る。 ==歴史== [[神功皇后]]が[[三韓征伐]]からの帰途、納祖の森に祭壇を設けて天神地祇を祀り、長年つき従った九州の臣たちと別れを惜しんだと伝えられる。このとき、人々が「またいつか尊顔を拝し奉らん」と口々に言い、この「いつか」が「飯塚」の名の由来であるという。 社伝では、その跡に作られたのが当社であるという。創建年代は不詳であるが、[[延文]]4年（[[1359年]]）には社殿が建立されていた。大正13年に県社に昇格した。 [[Category:福岡県の神社|のうそはちまんくう]] [[Category:府県社|のうそはちまんくう]] [[Category:別表神社|のうそはちまんくう]] {{shinto-stub}}

天神地祇| 社格=県社・別表神社| 例祭=[[4月15日]]・[[10月15日]]| 神事=[[放生会|人形感謝祭]]・夏越大祓(茅の輪神事)}} '''曩祖八幡宮'''（のうそはちまんぐう）は、[[福岡県]][[飯塚市]]にある[[神社]]である。旧[[社格]]は[[県社]]で、現在は[[神社本庁]]の[[別表神社]]。旧称'''納祖宮'''。 飯塚の[[鎮守神|鎮守]]として崇敬され、また子守りの神としても信仰されている。 ==祭神== [[応神天皇]]・[[仲哀天皇]]・[[神功皇后]]・[[武内宿禰]]および天神地祇を祀る。 ==歴史== [[神功皇后]]が[[三韓征伐]]からの帰途、納祖の森に祭壇を設けて天神地祇を祀り、長年つき従った九州の臣たちと別れを惜しんだと伝えられる。このとき、人々が「またいつか尊顔を拝し奉らん」と口々に言い、この「いつか」が「飯塚」の名の由来であるという。 社伝では、その跡に作られたのが当社であるという。創建年代は不詳であるが、[[延文]]4年（[[1359年]]）には社殿が建立されていた。大正13年に県社に昇格した。 == 祭典 == 月次祭　毎月一日 節分星祭　二月三日 人形感謝祭　五月第二日曜日 夏越大祓　六月三十日 == 外部リンク == *[http://nouso.or.jp/ 曩祖八幡宮 のうそはちまんぐう] {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:のうそはちまんくう}} [[Category:福岡県の神社]] [[Category:福岡県の旧県社]] [[Category:別表神社]] [[Category:八幡宮]] [[Category:飯塚市]] [[Category:飯塚市の歴史]]

revision.text:
{{draft}} {{infobox shinto shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = Noso Hachimangu.jpg | deity = [[Hachiman]] }} [[{{PAGENAME}}]] is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Japan]]. It is the main shrine of Iizuka in Fukuoka.{{Cite web |last=Ojisanjake |title=Noso Hachimangu |url=https://www.ojisanjake.com/2016/02/noso-hachimangu.html |access-date=2023-10-20 |language=en}} It is a [[Beppyo shrine]], or a shrine that is particularly notable in a certain way with a significant history to it.{{Cite web |title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー |url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/ |access-date=2023-04-02 |website=jinja-gosyuin.com}} It is a [[Hachiman shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to the [[kami]] [[Hachiman]]{{cite web |last=Motegi |first=Sadazumi |title=Shamei Bunpu (Shrine names and Distributions) |url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=776 |accessdate=23 March 2010 |publisher=Encyclopedia of Shinto |language=Japanese}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Noso Hachimangu | image = [[File:Noso Hachimangu.jpg|260px|Noso Hachimangu]]
Haiden | location = 2-3 Miyamachi, Iizuka City, Fukuoka Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|33.6411|N|130.6875|E|region:JP-40|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ubl|Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Chūai|Empress Jingū|Takenouchi no Sukune|Heavenly and Earthly Deities}} | type = Former Prefectural Shrine; {{ill|Betsu Hyōsha|ja|別表神社}} | festivals = {{ubl|April 15|October 15}} | rituals = {{ubl|Doll Gratitude festival (人形感謝祭)|Nagoshinoharae (Chinowa Ritual)}} | map = {{location map|Japan Fukuoka|label=Noso Hachimangu|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''Noso Hachimangu''' (曩祖八幡宮) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} in {{ill|Iizuka City|ja|飯塚市}}, Fukuoka Prefecture, Japan. It is a former {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社}} and currently listed as a {{ill|Betsu Hyōsha|ja|別表神社}} under the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|ja|神社本庁}}. The shrine was previously known as "Noso-no-Miya" (納祖宮). The shrine is revered as the guardian deity of Iizuka and is also worshipped as a protector of children. == Deities Enshrined == The shrine venerates the following deities: * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} * {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}} * {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} * {{ill|Takenouchi no Sukune|ja|武内宿禰}} * {{ill|Heavenly and Earthly Deities|ja|天神地祇}} == History == According to legend, {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} established an altar in the "Noso Forest" on her return from the {{ill|Sankan Seibatsu|ja|三韓征伐}} campaign to offer prayers to the Heavenly and Earthly Deities and to bid farewell to her loyal retainers from Kyushu. The name "Iizuka" is said to originate from this occasion, as the people proclaimed, "We hope to see your face again someday," with "itsuka" (いつか, someday) evolving into "Iizuka." The shrine’s records suggest its foundation occurred soon after this event, though the exact date is unknown. By the {{ill|Enbun|ja|延文}} period (1359), shrine buildings had been constructed. It was designated as a Prefectural Shrine in {{ill|Taishō|ja|大正}} 13 (1924). == festivals and Rituals == * **Monthly festival** (Tsukinamisai): 1st day of every month * **Setsubun Star festival**: February 3 * **Doll Gratitude festival**: 2nd Sunday in May * **Nagoshinoharae (Chinowa Ritual)**: June 30 == Notes == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{Official website|name=Noso Hachimangu}} {{draft}} {{infobox shinto shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = Noso Hachimangu.jpg | deity = [[Hachiman]] }} [[{{PAGENAME}}]] is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Japan]]. It is the main shrine of Iizuka in Fukuoka.{{Cite web |last=Ojisanjake |title=Noso Hachimangu |url=https://www.ojisanjake.com/2016/02/noso-hachimangu.html |access-date=2023-10-20 |language=en}} It is a [[Beppyo shrine]], or a shrine that is particularly notable in a certain way with a significant history to it.{{Cite web |title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー |url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/ |access-date=2023-04-02 |website=jinja-gosyuin.com}} It is a [[Hachiman shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to the [[kami]] [[Hachiman]]{{cite web |last=Motegi |first=Sadazumi |title=Shamei Bunpu (Shrine names and Distributions) |url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=776 |accessdate=23 March 2010 |publisher=Encyclopedia of Shinto |language=Japanese}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Noso Hachimangu | image = [[File:Noso Hachimangu.jpg|260px|Noso Hachimangu]]
Haiden | location = 2-3 Miyamachi, Iizuka City, Fukuoka Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|33.6411|N|130.6875|E|region:JP-40|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ubl|Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Chūai|Empress Jingū|Takenouchi no Sukune|Heavenly and Earthly Deities}} | type = Former Prefectural Shrine; {{ill|Betsu Hyōsha|ja|別表神社}} | festivals = {{ubl|April 15|October 15}} | rituals = {{ubl|Doll Gratitude festival (人形感謝祭)|Nagoshinoharae (Chinowa Ritual)}} | map = {{location map|Japan Fukuoka|label=Noso Hachimangu|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''Noso Hachimangu''' (曩祖八幡宮) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} in {{ill|Iizuka City|ja|飯塚市}}, Fukuoka Prefecture, Japan. It is a former {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社}} and currently listed as a {{ill|Betsu Hyōsha|ja|別表神社}} under the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|ja|神社本庁}}. The shrine was previously known as "Noso-no-Miya" (納祖宮). The shrine is revered as the guardian deity of Iizuka and is also worshipped as a protector of children. == Deities Enshrined == The shrine venerates the following deities: * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} * {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}} * {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} * {{ill|Takenouchi no Sukune|ja|武内宿禰}} * {{ill|Heavenly and Earthly Deities|ja|天神地祇}} == History == According to legend, {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} established an altar in the "Noso Forest" on her return from the {{ill|Sankan Seibatsu|ja|三韓征伐}} campaign to offer prayers to the Heavenly and Earthly Deities and to bid farewell to her loyal retainers from Kyushu. The name "Iizuka" is said to originate from this occasion, as the people proclaimed, "We hope to see your face again someday," with "itsuka" (いつか, someday) evolving into "Iizuka." The shrine’s records suggest its foundation occurred soon after this event, though the exact date is unknown. By the {{ill|Enbun|ja|延文}} period (1359), shrine buildings had been constructed. It was designated as a Prefectural Shrine in {{ill|Taishō|ja|大正}} 13 (1924). == festivals and Rituals == * **Monthly festival** (Tsukinamisai): 1st day of every month * **Setsubun Star festival**: February 3 * **Doll Gratitude festival**: 2nd Sunday in May * **Nagoshinoharae (Chinowa Ritual)**: June 30 == Notes == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{Official website|name=Noso Hachimangu}} Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox shinto shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = Noso Hachimangu.jpg | deity = [[Hachiman]] }} [[{{PAGENAME}}]] is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Japan]]. It is the main shrine of Iizuka in Fukuoka.{{Cite web |last=Ojisanjake |title=Noso Hachimangu |url=https://www.ojisanjake.com/2016/02/noso-hachimangu.html |access-date=2023-10-20 |language=en}} It is a [[Beppyo shrine]], or a shrine that is particularly notable in a certain way with a significant history to it.{{Cite web |title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー |url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/ |access-date=2023-04-02 |website=jinja-gosyuin.com}} It is a [[Hachiman shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to the [[kami]] [[Hachiman]]{{cite web |last=Motegi |first=Sadazumi |title=Shamei Bunpu (Shrine names and Distributions) |url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=776 |accessdate=23 March 2010 |publisher=Encyclopedia of Shinto |language=Japanese}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Noso Hachimangu | image = [[File:Noso Hachimangu.jpg|260px|Noso Hachimangu]]
Haiden | location = 2-3 Miyamachi, Iizuka City, Fukuoka Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|33.6411|N|130.6875|E|region:JP-40|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ubl|Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Chūai|Empress Jingū|Takenouchi no Sukune|Heavenly and Earthly Deities}} | type = Former Prefectural Shrine; {{ill|Betsu Hyōsha|ja|別表神社}} | festivals = {{ubl|April 15|October 15}} | rituals = {{ubl|Doll Gratitude festival (人形感謝祭)|Nagoshinoharae (Chinowa Ritual)}} | map = {{location map|Japan Fukuoka|label=Noso Hachimangu|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''Noso Hachimangu''' (曩祖八幡宮) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} in {{ill|Iizuka City|ja|飯塚市}}, Fukuoka Prefecture, Japan. It is a former {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社}} and currently listed as a {{ill|Betsu Hyōsha|ja|別表神社}} under the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|ja|神社本庁}}. The shrine was previously known as "Noso-no-Miya" (納祖宮). The shrine is revered as the guardian deity of Iizuka and is also worshipped as a protector of children. == Deities Enshrined == The shrine venerates the following deities: * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} * {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}} * {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} * {{ill|Takenouchi no Sukune|ja|武内宿禰}} * {{ill|Heavenly and Earthly Deities|ja|天神地祇}} == History == According to legend, {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} established an altar in the "Noso Forest" on her return from the {{ill|Sankan Seibatsu|ja|三韓征伐}} campaign to offer prayers to the Heavenly and Earthly Deities and to bid farewell to her loyal retainers from Kyushu. The name "Iizuka" is said to originate from this occasion, as the people proclaimed, "We hope to see your face again someday," with "itsuka" (いつか, someday) evolving into "Iizuka." The shrine’s records suggest its foundation occurred soon after this event, though the exact date is unknown. By the {{ill|Enbun|ja|延文}} period (1359), shrine buildings had been constructed. It was designated as a Prefectural Shrine in {{ill|Taishō|ja|大正}} 13 (1924). == festivals and Rituals == * **Monthly festival** (Tsukinamisai): 1st day of every month * **Setsubun Star festival**: February 3 * **Doll Gratitude festival**: 2nd Sunday in May * **Nagoshinoharae (Chinowa Ritual)**: June 30 == Notes == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{Official website|name=Noso Hachimangu}} Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox shinto shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = Noso Hachimangu.jpg | deity = [[Hachiman]] }} [[{{PAGENAME}}]] is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Japan]]. It is the main shrine of Iizuka in Fukuoka.{{Cite web |last=Ojisanjake |title=Noso Hachimangu |url=https://www.ojisanjake.com/2016/02/noso-hachimangu.html |access-date=2023-10-20 |language=en}} It is a [[Beppyo shrine]], or a shrine that is particularly notable in a certain way with a significant history to it.{{Cite web |title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー |url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/ |access-date=2023-04-02 |website=jinja-gosyuin.com}} It is a [[Hachiman shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to the [[kami]] [[Hachiman]]{{cite web |last=Motegi |first=Sadazumi |title=Shamei Bunpu (Shrine names and Distributions) |url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=776 |accessdate=23 March 2010 |publisher=Encyclopedia of Shinto |language=Japanese}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Noso Hachimangu | image = [[File:Noso Hachimangu.jpg|260px|Noso Hachimangu]]
Haiden | location = 2-3 Miyamachi, Iizuka City, Fukuoka Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|33.6411|N|130.6875|E|region:JP-40|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ubl|Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Chūai|Empress Jingū|Takenouchi no Sukune|Heavenly and Earthly Deities}} | type = Former Prefectural Shrine; {{ill|Betsu Hyōsha|ja|別表神社}} | festivals = {{ubl|April 15|October 15}} | rituals = {{ubl|Doll Gratitude festival (人形感謝祭)|Nagoshinoharae (Chinowa Ritual)}} | map = {{location map|Japan Fukuoka|label=Noso Hachimangu|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''Noso Hachimangu''' (曩祖八幡宮) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} in {{ill|Iizuka City|ja|飯塚市}}, Fukuoka Prefecture, Japan. It is a former {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社}} and currently listed as a {{ill|Betsu Hyōsha|ja|別表神社}} under the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|ja|神社本庁}}. The shrine was previously known as "Noso-no-Miya" (納祖宮). The shrine is revered as the guardian deity of Iizuka and is also worshipped as a protector of children. == Deities Enshrined == The shrine venerates the following deities: * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} * {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}} * {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} * {{ill|Takenouchi no Sukune|ja|武内宿禰}} * {{ill|Heavenly and Earthly Deities|ja|天神地祇}} == History == According to legend, {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} established an altar in the "Noso Forest" on her return from the {{ill|Sankan Seibatsu|ja|三韓征伐}} campaign to offer prayers to the Heavenly and Earthly Deities and to bid farewell to her loyal retainers from Kyushu. The name "Iizuka" is said to originate from this occasion, as the people proclaimed, "We hope to see your face again someday," with "itsuka" (いつか, someday) evolving into "Iizuka." The shrine’s records suggest its foundation occurred soon after this event, though the exact date is unknown. By the {{ill|Enbun|ja|延文}} period (1359), shrine buildings had been constructed. It was designated as a Prefectural Shrine in {{ill|Taishō|ja|大正}} 13 (1924). == festivals and Rituals == * **Monthly festival** (Tsukinamisai): 1st day of every month * **Setsubun Star festival**: February 3 * **Doll Gratitude festival**: 2nd Sunday in May * **Nagoshinoharae (Chinowa Ritual)**: June 30 == Notes == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{Official website|name=Noso Hachimangu}} Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox shinto shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = Noso Hachimangu.jpg | deity = [[Hachiman]] }} [[{{PAGENAME}}]] is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Japan]]. It is the main shrine of Iizuka in Fukuoka.{{Cite web |last=Ojisanjake |title=Noso Hachimangu |url=https://www.ojisanjake.com/2016/02/noso-hachimangu.html |access-date=2023-10-20 |language=en}} It is a [[Beppyo shrine]], or a shrine that is particularly notable in a certain way with a significant history to it.{{Cite web |title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー |url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/ |access-date=2023-04-02 |website=jinja-gosyuin.com}} It is a [[Hachiman shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to the [[kami]] [[Hachiman]]{{cite web |last=Motegi |first=Sadazumi |title=Shamei Bunpu (Shrine names and Distributions) |url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=776 |accessdate=23 March 2010 |publisher=Encyclopedia of Shinto |language=Japanese}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Noso Hachimangu | image = [[File:Noso Hachimangu.jpg|260px|Noso Hachimangu]]
Haiden | location = 2-3 Miyamachi, Iizuka City, Fukuoka Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|33.6411|N|130.6875|E|region:JP-40|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ubl|Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Chūai|Empress Jingū|Takenouchi no Sukune|Heavenly and Earthly Deities}} | type = Former Prefectural Shrine; {{ill|Betsu Hyōsha|ja|別表神社}} | festivals = {{ubl|April 15|October 15}} | rituals = {{ubl|Doll Gratitude festival (人形感謝祭)|Nagoshinoharae (Chinowa Ritual)}} | map = {{location map|Japan Fukuoka|label=Noso Hachimangu|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''Noso Hachimangu''' (曩祖八幡宮) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} in {{ill|Iizuka City|ja|飯塚市}}, Fukuoka Prefecture, Japan. It is a former {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社}} and currently listed as a {{ill|Betsu Hyōsha|ja|別表神社}} under the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|ja|神社本庁}}. The shrine was previously known as "Noso-no-Miya" (納祖宮). The shrine is revered as the guardian deity of Iizuka and is also worshipped as a protector of children. == Deities Enshrined == The shrine venerates the following deities: * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} * {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}} * {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} * {{ill|Takenouchi no Sukune|ja|武内宿禰}} * {{ill|Heavenly and Earthly Deities|ja|天神地祇}} == History == According to legend, {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} established an altar in the "Noso Forest" on her return from the {{ill|Sankan Seibatsu|ja|三韓征伐}} campaign to offer prayers to the Heavenly and Earthly Deities and to bid farewell to her loyal retainers from Kyushu. The name "Iizuka" is said to originate from this occasion, as the people proclaimed, "We hope to see your face again someday," with "itsuka" (いつか, someday) evolving into "Iizuka." The shrine’s records suggest its foundation occurred soon after this event, though the exact date is unknown. By the {{ill|Enbun|ja|延文}} period (1359), shrine buildings had been constructed. It was designated as a Prefectural Shrine in {{ill|Taishō|ja|大正}} 13 (1924). == festivals and Rituals == * **Monthly festival** (Tsukinamisai): 1st day of every month * **Setsubun Star festival**: February 3 * **Doll Gratitude festival**: 2nd Sunday in May * **Nagoshinoharae (Chinowa Ritual)**: June 30 == Notes == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{Official website|name=Noso Hachimangu}} Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox shinto shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = Noso Hachimangu.jpg | deity = [[Hachiman]] }} [[{{PAGENAME}}]] is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Japan]]. It is the main shrine of Iizuka in Fukuoka.{{Cite web |last=Ojisanjake |title=Noso Hachimangu |url=https://www.ojisanjake.com/2016/02/noso-hachimangu.html |access-date=2023-10-20 |language=en}} It is a [[Beppyo shrine]], or a shrine that is particularly notable in a certain way with a significant history to it.{{Cite web |title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー |url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/ |access-date=2023-04-02 |website=jinja-gosyuin.com}} It is a [[Hachiman shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to the [[kami]] [[Hachiman]]{{cite web |last=Motegi |first=Sadazumi |title=Shamei Bunpu (Shrine names and Distributions) |url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=776 |accessdate=23 March 2010 |publisher=Encyclopedia of Shinto |language=Japanese}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Noso Hachimangu | image = [[File:Noso Hachimangu.jpg|260px|Noso Hachimangu]]
Haiden | location = 2-3 Miyamachi, Iizuka City, Fukuoka Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|33.6411|N|130.6875|E|region:JP-40|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ubl|Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Chūai|Empress Jingū|Takenouchi no Sukune|Heavenly and Earthly Deities}} | type = Former Prefectural Shrine; {{ill|Betsu Hyōsha|ja|別表神社}} | festivals = {{ubl|April 15|October 15}} | rituals = {{ubl|Doll Gratitude festival (人形感謝祭)|Nagoshinoharae (Chinowa Ritual)}} | map = {{location map|Japan Fukuoka|label=Noso Hachimangu|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''Noso Hachimangu''' (曩祖八幡宮) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} in {{ill|Iizuka City|ja|飯塚市}}, Fukuoka Prefecture, Japan. It is a former {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社}} and currently listed as a {{ill|Betsu Hyōsha|ja|別表神社}} under the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|ja|神社本庁}}. The shrine was previously known as "Noso-no-Miya" (納祖宮). The shrine is revered as the guardian deity of Iizuka and is also worshipped as a protector of children. == Deities Enshrined == The shrine venerates the following deities: * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} * {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}} * {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} * {{ill|Takenouchi no Sukune|ja|武内宿禰}} * {{ill|Heavenly and Earthly Deities|ja|天神地祇}} == History == According to legend, {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} established an altar in the "Noso Forest" on her return from the {{ill|Sankan Seibatsu|ja|三韓征伐}} campaign to offer prayers to the Heavenly and Earthly Deities and to bid farewell to her loyal retainers from Kyushu. The name "Iizuka" is said to originate from this occasion, as the people proclaimed, "We hope to see your face again someday," with "itsuka" (いつか, someday) evolving into "Iizuka." The shrine’s records suggest its foundation occurred soon after this event, though the exact date is unknown. By the {{ill|Enbun|ja|延文}} period (1359), shrine buildings had been constructed. It was designated as a Prefectural Shrine in {{ill|Taishō|ja|大正}} 13 (1924). == festivals and Rituals == * **Monthly festival** (Tsukinamisai): 1st day of every month * **Setsubun Star festival**: February 3 * **Doll Gratitude festival**: 2nd Sunday in May * **Nagoshinoharae (Chinowa Ritual)**: June 30 == Notes == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{Official website|name=Noso Hachimangu}} Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox shinto shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = Noso Hachimangu.jpg | deity = [[Hachiman]] }} [[{{PAGENAME}}]] is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Japan]]. It is the main shrine of Iizuka in Fukuoka.{{Cite web |last=Ojisanjake |title=Noso Hachimangu |url=https://www.ojisanjake.com/2016/02/noso-hachimangu.html |access-date=2023-10-20 |language=en}} It is a [[Beppyo shrine]], or a shrine that is particularly notable in a certain way with a significant history to it.{{Cite web |title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー |url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/ |access-date=2023-04-02 |website=jinja-gosyuin.com}} It is a [[Hachiman shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to the [[kami]] [[Hachiman]]{{cite web |last=Motegi |first=Sadazumi |title=Shamei Bunpu (Shrine names and Distributions) |url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=776 |accessdate=23 March 2010 |publisher=Encyclopedia of Shinto |language=Japanese}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Noso Hachimangu | image = [[File:Noso Hachimangu.jpg|260px|Noso Hachimangu]]
Haiden | location = 2-3 Miyamachi, Iizuka City, Fukuoka Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|33.6411|N|130.6875|E|region:JP-40|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ubl|Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Chūai|Empress Jingū|Takenouchi no Sukune|Heavenly and Earthly Deities}} | type = Former Prefectural Shrine; {{ill|Betsu Hyōsha|ja|別表神社}} | festivals = {{ubl|April 15|October 15}} | rituals = {{ubl|Doll Gratitude festival (人形感謝祭)|Nagoshinoharae (Chinowa Ritual)}} | map = {{location map|Japan Fukuoka|label=Noso Hachimangu|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''Noso Hachimangu''' (曩祖八幡宮) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} in {{ill|Iizuka City|ja|飯塚市}}, Fukuoka Prefecture, Japan. It is a former {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社}} and currently listed as a {{ill|Betsu Hyōsha|ja|別表神社}} under the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|ja|神社本庁}}. The shrine was previously known as "Noso-no-Miya" (納祖宮). The shrine is revered as the guardian deity of Iizuka and is also worshipped as a protector of children. == Deities Enshrined == The shrine venerates the following deities: * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} * {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}} * {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} * {{ill|Takenouchi no Sukune|ja|武内宿禰}} * {{ill|Heavenly and Earthly Deities|ja|天神地祇}} == History == According to legend, {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} established an altar in the "Noso Forest" on her return from the {{ill|Sankan Seibatsu|ja|三韓征伐}} campaign to offer prayers to the Heavenly and Earthly Deities and to bid farewell to her loyal retainers from Kyushu. The name "Iizuka" is said to originate from this occasion, as the people proclaimed, "We hope to see your face again someday," with "itsuka" (いつか, someday) evolving into "Iizuka." The shrine’s records suggest its foundation occurred soon after this event, though the exact date is unknown. By the {{ill|Enbun|ja|延文}} period (1359), shrine buildings had been constructed. It was designated as a Prefectural Shrine in {{ill|Taishō|ja|大正}} 13 (1924). == festivals and Rituals == * **Monthly festival** (Tsukinamisai): 1st day of every month * **Setsubun Star festival**: February 3 * **Doll Gratitude festival**: 2nd Sunday in May * **Nagoshinoharae (Chinowa Ritual)**: June 30 == Notes == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{Official website|name=Noso Hachimangu}} Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox shinto shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = Noso Hachimangu.jpg | deity = [[Hachiman]] }} [[{{PAGENAME}}]] is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Japan]]. It is the main shrine of Iizuka in Fukuoka.{{Cite web |last=Ojisanjake |title=Noso Hachimangu |url=https://www.ojisanjake.com/2016/02/noso-hachimangu.html |access-date=2023-10-20 |language=en}} It is a [[Beppyo shrine]], or a shrine that is particularly notable in a certain way with a significant history to it.{{Cite web |title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー |url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/ |access-date=2023-04-02 |website=jinja-gosyuin.com}} It is a [[Hachiman shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to the [[kami]] [[Hachiman]]{{cite web |last=Motegi |first=Sadazumi |title=Shamei Bunpu (Shrine names and Distributions) |url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=776 |accessdate=23 March 2010 |publisher=Encyclopedia of Shinto |language=Japanese}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Noso Hachimangu | image = [[File:Noso Hachimangu.jpg|260px|Noso Hachimangu]]
Haiden | location = 2-3 Miyamachi, Iizuka City, Fukuoka Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|33.6411|N|130.6875|E|region:JP-40|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ubl|Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Chūai|Empress Jingū|Takenouchi no Sukune|Heavenly and Earthly Deities}} | type = Former Prefectural Shrine; {{ill|Betsu Hyōsha|ja|別表神社}} | festivals = {{ubl|April 15|October 15}} | rituals = {{ubl|Doll Gratitude festival (人形感謝祭)|Nagoshinoharae (Chinowa Ritual)}} | map = {{location map|Japan Fukuoka|label=Noso Hachimangu|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''Noso Hachimangu''' (曩祖八幡宮) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} in {{ill|Iizuka City|ja|飯塚市}}, Fukuoka Prefecture, Japan. It is a former {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社}} and currently listed as a {{ill|Betsu Hyōsha|ja|別表神社}} under the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|ja|神社本庁}}. The shrine was previously known as "Noso-no-Miya" (納祖宮). The shrine is revered as the guardian deity of Iizuka and is also worshipped as a protector of children. == Deities Enshrined == The shrine venerates the following deities: * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} * {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}} * {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} * {{ill|Takenouchi no Sukune|ja|武内宿禰}} * {{ill|Heavenly and Earthly Deities|ja|天神地祇}} == History == According to legend, {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} established an altar in the "Noso Forest" on her return from the {{ill|Sankan Seibatsu|ja|三韓征伐}} campaign to offer prayers to the Heavenly and Earthly Deities and to bid farewell to her loyal retainers from Kyushu. The name "Iizuka" is said to originate from this occasion, as the people proclaimed, "We hope to see your face again someday," with "itsuka" (いつか, someday) evolving into "Iizuka." The shrine’s records suggest its foundation occurred soon after this event, though the exact date is unknown. By the {{ill|Enbun|ja|延文}} period (1359), shrine buildings had been constructed. It was designated as a Prefectural Shrine in {{ill|Taishō|ja|大正}} 13 (1924). == festivals and Rituals == * **Monthly festival** (Tsukinamisai): 1st day of every month * **Setsubun Star festival**: February 3 * **Doll Gratitude festival**: 2nd Sunday in May * **Nagoshinoharae (Chinowa Ritual)**: June 30 == Notes == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{Official website|name=Noso Hachimangu}} Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox shinto shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = Noso Hachimangu.jpg | deity = [[Hachiman]] }} [[{{PAGENAME}}]] is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Japan]]. It is the main shrine of Iizuka in Fukuoka.{{Cite web |last=Ojisanjake |title=Noso Hachimangu |url=https://www.ojisanjake.com/2016/02/noso-hachimangu.html |access-date=2023-10-20 |language=en}} It is a [[Beppyo shrine]], or a shrine that is particularly notable in a certain way with a significant history to it.{{Cite web |title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー |url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/ |access-date=2023-04-02 |website=jinja-gosyuin.com}} It is a [[Hachiman shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to the [[kami]] [[Hachiman]]{{cite web |last=Motegi |first=Sadazumi |title=Shamei Bunpu (Shrine names and Distributions) |url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=776 |accessdate=23 March 2010 |publisher=Encyclopedia of Shinto |language=Japanese}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Noso Hachimangu | image = [[File:Noso Hachimangu.jpg|260px|Noso Hachimangu]]
Haiden | location = 2-3 Miyamachi, Iizuka City, Fukuoka Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|33.6411|N|130.6875|E|region:JP-40|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ubl|Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Chūai|Empress Jingū|Takenouchi no Sukune|Heavenly and Earthly Deities}} | type = Former Prefectural Shrine; {{ill|Betsu Hyōsha|ja|別表神社}} | festivals = {{ubl|April 15|October 15}} | rituals = {{ubl|Doll Gratitude festival (人形感謝祭)|Nagoshinoharae (Chinowa Ritual)}} | map = {{location map|Japan Fukuoka|label=Noso Hachimangu|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''Noso Hachimangu''' (曩祖八幡宮) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} in {{ill|Iizuka City|ja|飯塚市}}, Fukuoka Prefecture, Japan. It is a former {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社}} and currently listed as a {{ill|Betsu Hyōsha|ja|別表神社}} under the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|ja|神社本庁}}. The shrine was previously known as "Noso-no-Miya" (納祖宮). The shrine is revered as the guardian deity of Iizuka and is also worshipped as a protector of children. == Deities Enshrined == The shrine venerates the following deities: * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} * {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}} * {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} * {{ill|Takenouchi no Sukune|ja|武内宿禰}} * {{ill|Heavenly and Earthly Deities|ja|天神地祇}} == History == According to legend, {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} established an altar in the "Noso Forest" on her return from the {{ill|Sankan Seibatsu|ja|三韓征伐}} campaign to offer prayers to the Heavenly and Earthly Deities and to bid farewell to her loyal retainers from Kyushu. The name "Iizuka" is said to originate from this occasion, as the people proclaimed, "We hope to see your face again someday," with "itsuka" (いつか, someday) evolving into "Iizuka." The shrine’s records suggest its foundation occurred soon after this event, though the exact date is unknown. By the {{ill|Enbun|ja|延文}} period (1359), shrine buildings had been constructed. It was designated as a Prefectural Shrine in {{ill|Taishō|ja|大正}} 13 (1924). == festivals and Rituals == * **Monthly festival** (Tsukinamisai): 1st day of every month * **Setsubun Star festival**: February 3 * **Doll Gratitude festival**: 2nd Sunday in May * **Nagoshinoharae (Chinowa Ritual)**: June 30 == Notes == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{Official website|name=Noso Hachimangu}} Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox shinto shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = Noso Hachimangu.jpg | deity = [[Hachiman]] }} [[{{PAGENAME}}]] is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Japan]]. It is the main shrine of Iizuka in Fukuoka.{{Cite web |last=Ojisanjake |title=Noso Hachimangu |url=https://www.ojisanjake.com/2016/02/noso-hachimangu.html |access-date=2023-10-20 |language=en}} It is a [[Beppyo shrine]], or a shrine that is particularly notable in a certain way with a significant history to it.{{Cite web |title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー |url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/ |access-date=2023-04-02 |website=jinja-gosyuin.com}} It is a [[Hachiman shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to the [[kami]] [[Hachiman]]{{cite web |last=Motegi |first=Sadazumi |title=Shamei Bunpu (Shrine names and Distributions) |url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=776 |accessdate=23 March 2010 |publisher=Encyclopedia of Shinto |language=Japanese}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Noso Hachimangu | image = [[File:Noso Hachimangu.jpg|260px|Noso Hachimangu]]
Haiden | location = 2-3 Miyamachi, Iizuka City, Fukuoka Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|33.6411|N|130.6875|E|region:JP-40|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ubl|Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Chūai|Empress Jingū|Takenouchi no Sukune|Heavenly and Earthly Deities}} | type = Former Prefectural Shrine; {{ill|Betsu Hyōsha|ja|別表神社}} | festivals = {{ubl|April 15|October 15}} | rituals = {{ubl|Doll Gratitude festival (人形感謝祭)|Nagoshinoharae (Chinowa Ritual)}} | map = {{location map|Japan Fukuoka|label=Noso Hachimangu|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''Noso Hachimangu''' (曩祖八幡宮) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} in {{ill|Iizuka City|ja|飯塚市}}, Fukuoka Prefecture, Japan. It is a former {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社}} and currently listed as a {{ill|Betsu Hyōsha|ja|別表神社}} under the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|ja|神社本庁}}. The shrine was previously known as "Noso-no-Miya" (納祖宮). The shrine is revered as the guardian deity of Iizuka and is also worshipped as a protector of children. == Deities Enshrined == The shrine venerates the following deities: * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} * {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}} * {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} * {{ill|Takenouchi no Sukune|ja|武内宿禰}} * {{ill|Heavenly and Earthly Deities|ja|天神地祇}} == History == According to legend, {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} established an altar in the "Noso Forest" on her return from the {{ill|Sankan Seibatsu|ja|三韓征伐}} campaign to offer prayers to the Heavenly and Earthly Deities and to bid farewell to her loyal retainers from Kyushu. The name "Iizuka" is said to originate from this occasion, as the people proclaimed, "We hope to see your face again someday," with "itsuka" (いつか, someday) evolving into "Iizuka." The shrine’s records suggest its foundation occurred soon after this event, though the exact date is unknown. By the {{ill|Enbun|ja|延文}} period (1359), shrine buildings had been constructed. It was designated as a Prefectural Shrine in {{ill|Taishō|ja|大正}} 13 (1924). == festivals and Rituals == * **Monthly festival** (Tsukinamisai): 1st day of every month * **Setsubun Star festival**: February 3 * **Doll Gratitude festival**: 2nd Sunday in May * **Nagoshinoharae (Chinowa Ritual)**: June 30 == Notes == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{Official website|name=Noso Hachimangu}} It is a former {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社}} and currently listed as a {{ill|Betsu Hyōsha|ja|別表神社}} under the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|ja|神社本庁}}. The shrine was previously known as "Noso-no-Miya" (納祖宮). The shrine is revered as the guardian deity of Iizuka and is also worshipped as a protector of children. == Deities Enshrined == The shrine venerates the following deities: * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} * {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}} * {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} * {{ill|Takenouchi no Sukune|ja|武内宿禰}} * {{ill|Heavenly and Earthly Deities|ja|天神地祇}} == History == According to legend, {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} established an altar in the "Noso Forest" on her return from the {{ill|Sankan Seibatsu|ja|三韓征伐}} campaign to offer prayers to the Heavenly and Earthly Deities and to bid farewell to her loyal retainers from Kyushu. The name "Iizuka" is said to originate from this occasion, as the people proclaimed, "We hope to see your face again someday," with "itsuka" (いつか, someday) evolving into "Iizuka." The shrine’s records suggest its foundation occurred soon after this event, though the exact date is unknown. By the {{ill|Enbun|ja|延文}} period (1359), shrine buildings had been constructed. It was designated as a Prefectural Shrine in {{ill|Taishō|ja|大正}} 13 (1924). == festivals and Rituals == * **Monthly festival** (Tsukinamisai): 1st day of every month * **Setsubun Star festival**: February 3 * **Doll Gratitude festival**: 2nd Sunday in May * **Nagoshinoharae (Chinowa Ritual)**: June 30 == Notes == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{Official website|name=Noso Hachimangu}} Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox shinto shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = Noso Hachimangu.jpg | deity = [[Hachiman]] }} [[{{PAGENAME}}]] is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Japan]]. It is the main shrine of Iizuka in Fukuoka.{{Cite web |last=Ojisanjake |title=Noso Hachimangu |url=https://www.ojisanjake.com/2016/02/noso-hachimangu.html |access-date=2023-10-20 |language=en}} It is a [[Beppyo shrine]], or a shrine that is particularly notable in a certain way with a significant history to it.{{Cite web |title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー |url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/ |access-date=2023-04-02 |website=jinja-gosyuin.com}} It is a [[Hachiman shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to the [[kami]] [[Hachiman]]{{cite web |last=Motegi |first=Sadazumi |title=Shamei Bunpu (Shrine names and Distributions) |url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=776 |accessdate=23 March 2010 |publisher=Encyclopedia of Shinto |language=Japanese}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Noso Hachimangu | image = [[File:Noso Hachimangu.jpg|260px|Noso Hachimangu]]
Haiden | location = 2-3 Miyamachi, Iizuka City, Fukuoka Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|33.6411|N|130.6875|E|region:JP-40|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ubl|Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Chūai|Empress Jingū|Takenouchi no Sukune|Heavenly and Earthly Deities}} | type = Former Prefectural Shrine; {{ill|Betsu Hyōsha|ja|別表神社}} | festivals = {{ubl|April 15|October 15}} | rituals = {{ubl|Doll Gratitude festival (人形感謝祭)|Nagoshinoharae (Chinowa Ritual)}} | map = {{location map|Japan Fukuoka|label=Noso Hachimangu|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''Noso Hachimangu''' (曩祖八幡宮) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} in {{ill|Iizuka City|ja|飯塚市}}, Fukuoka Prefecture, Japan. It is a former {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社}} and currently listed as a {{ill|Betsu Hyōsha|ja|別表神社}} under the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|ja|神社本庁}}. The shrine was previously known as "Noso-no-Miya" (納祖宮). The shrine is revered as the guardian deity of Iizuka and is also worshipped as a protector of children. == Deities Enshrined == The shrine venerates the following deities: * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} * {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}} * {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} * {{ill|Takenouchi no Sukune|ja|武内宿禰}} * {{ill|Heavenly and Earthly Deities|ja|天神地祇}} == History == According to legend, {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} established an altar in the "Noso Forest" on her return from the {{ill|Sankan Seibatsu|ja|三韓征伐}} campaign to offer prayers to the Heavenly and Earthly Deities and to bid farewell to her loyal retainers from Kyushu. The name "Iizuka" is said to originate from this occasion, as the people proclaimed, "We hope to see your face again someday," with "itsuka" (いつか, someday) evolving into "Iizuka." The shrine’s records suggest its foundation occurred soon after this event, though the exact date is unknown. By the {{ill|Enbun|ja|延文}} period (1359), shrine buildings had been constructed. It was designated as a Prefectural Shrine in {{ill|Taishō|ja|大正}} 13 (1924). == festivals and Rituals == * **Monthly festival** (Tsukinamisai): 1st day of every month * **Setsubun Star festival**: February 3 * **Doll Gratitude festival**: 2nd Sunday in May * **Nagoshinoharae (Chinowa Ritual)**: June 30 == Notes == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{Official website|name=Noso Hachimangu}} [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox shinto shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = Noso Hachimangu.jpg | deity = [[Hachiman]] }} [[{{PAGENAME}}]] is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Japan]]. It is the main shrine of Iizuka in Fukuoka.{{Cite web |last=Ojisanjake |title=Noso Hachimangu |url=https://www.ojisanjake.com/2016/02/noso-hachimangu.html |access-date=2023-10-20 |language=en}} It is a [[Beppyo shrine]], or a shrine that is particularly notable in a certain way with a significant history to it.{{Cite web |title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー |url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/ |access-date=2023-04-02 |website=jinja-gosyuin.com}} It is a [[Hachiman shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to the [[kami]] [[Hachiman]]{{cite web |last=Motegi |first=Sadazumi |title=Shamei Bunpu (Shrine names and Distributions) |url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=776 |accessdate=23 March 2010 |publisher=Encyclopedia of Shinto |language=Japanese}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Noso Hachimangu | image = [[File:Noso Hachimangu.jpg|260px|Noso Hachimangu]]
Haiden | location = 2-3 Miyamachi, Iizuka City, Fukuoka Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|33.6411|N|130.6875|E|region:JP-40|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ubl|Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Chūai|Empress Jingū|Takenouchi no Sukune|Heavenly and Earthly Deities}} | type = Former Prefectural Shrine; {{ill|Betsu Hyōsha|ja|別表神社}} | festivals = {{ubl|April 15|October 15}} | rituals = {{ubl|Doll Gratitude festival (人形感謝祭)|Nagoshinoharae (Chinowa Ritual)}} | map = {{location map|Japan Fukuoka|label=Noso Hachimangu|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''Noso Hachimangu''' (曩祖八幡宮) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} in {{ill|Iizuka City|ja|飯塚市}}, Fukuoka Prefecture, Japan. It is a former {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社}} and currently listed as a {{ill|Betsu Hyōsha|ja|別表神社}} under the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|ja|神社本庁}}. The shrine was previously known as "Noso-no-Miya" (納祖宮). The shrine is revered as the guardian deity of Iizuka and is also worshipped as a protector of children. == Deities Enshrined == The shrine venerates the following deities: * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} * {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}} * {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} * {{ill|Takenouchi no Sukune|ja|武内宿禰}} * {{ill|Heavenly and Earthly Deities|ja|天神地祇}} == History == According to legend, {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} established an altar in the "Noso Forest" on her return from the {{ill|Sankan Seibatsu|ja|三韓征伐}} campaign to offer prayers to the Heavenly and Earthly Deities and to bid farewell to her loyal retainers from Kyushu. The name "Iizuka" is said to originate from this occasion, as the people proclaimed, "We hope to see your face again someday," with "itsuka" (いつか, someday) evolving into "Iizuka." The shrine’s records suggest its foundation occurred soon after this event, though the exact date is unknown. By the {{ill|Enbun|ja|延文}} period (1359), shrine buildings had been constructed. It was designated as a Prefectural Shrine in {{ill|Taishō|ja|大正}} 13 (1924). == festivals and Rituals == * **Monthly festival** (Tsukinamisai): 1st day of every month * **Setsubun Star festival**: February 3 * **Doll Gratitude festival**: 2nd Sunday in May * **Nagoshinoharae (Chinowa Ritual)**: June 30 == Notes == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{Official website|name=Noso Hachimangu}} Haiden | location = 2-3 Miyamachi, Iizuka City, Fukuoka Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|33.6411|N|130.6875|E|region:JP-40|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ubl|Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Chūai|Empress Jingū|Takenouchi no Sukune|Heavenly and Earthly Deities}} | type = Former Prefectural Shrine; {{ill|Betsu Hyōsha|ja|別表神社}} | festivals = {{ubl|April 15|October 15}} | rituals = {{ubl|Doll Gratitude festival (人形感謝祭)|Nagoshinoharae (Chinowa Ritual)}} | map = {{location map|Japan Fukuoka|label=Noso Hachimangu|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''Noso Hachimangu''' (曩祖八幡宮) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} in {{ill|Iizuka City|ja|飯塚市}}, Fukuoka Prefecture, Japan. It is a former {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社}} and currently listed as a {{ill|Betsu Hyōsha|ja|別表神社}} under the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|ja|神社本庁}}. The shrine was previously known as "Noso-no-Miya" (納祖宮). The shrine is revered as the guardian deity of Iizuka and is also worshipped as a protector of children. == Deities Enshrined == The shrine venerates the following deities: * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} * {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}} * {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} * {{ill|Takenouchi no Sukune|ja|武内宿禰}} * {{ill|Heavenly and Earthly Deities|ja|天神地祇}} == History == According to legend, {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} established an altar in the "Noso Forest" on her return from the {{ill|Sankan Seibatsu|ja|三韓征伐}} campaign to offer prayers to the Heavenly and Earthly Deities and to bid farewell to her loyal retainers from Kyushu. The name "Iizuka" is said to originate from this occasion, as the people proclaimed, "We hope to see your face again someday," with "itsuka" (いつか, someday) evolving into "Iizuka." The shrine’s records suggest its foundation occurred soon after this event, though the exact date is unknown. By the {{ill|Enbun|ja|延文}} period (1359), shrine buildings had been constructed. It was designated as a Prefectural Shrine in {{ill|Taishō|ja|大正}} 13 (1924). == festivals and Rituals == * **Monthly festival** (Tsukinamisai): 1st day of every month * **Setsubun Star festival**: February 3 * **Doll Gratitude festival**: 2nd Sunday in May * **Nagoshinoharae (Chinowa Ritual)**: June 30 == Notes == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{Official website|name=Noso Hachimangu}} {{draft}} {{Promising draft|reason=the high amount of work put into it over a long time, see edit history}} {{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Noso Hachimangu]][[en:draft:Noso Hachimangu]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Noso Hachimangu|Noso Hachimangu]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox shinto shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = Noso Hachimangu.jpg | deity = [[Hachiman]] }} [[{{PAGENAME}}]] is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Japan]]. It is the main shrine of Iizuka in Fukuoka.{{Cite web |last=Ojisanjake |title=Noso Hachimangu |url=https://www.ojisanjake.com/2016/02/noso-hachimangu.html |access-date=2023-10-20 |language=en}} It is a [[Beppyo shrine]], or a shrine that is particularly notable in a certain way with a significant history to it.{{Cite web |title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー |url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/ |access-date=2023-04-02 |website=jinja-gosyuin.com}} It is a [[Hachiman shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to the [[kami]] [[Hachiman]]{{cite web |last=Motegi |first=Sadazumi |title=Shamei Bunpu (Shrine names and Distributions) |url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=776 |accessdate=23 March 2010 |publisher=Encyclopedia of Shinto |language=Japanese}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Noso Hachimangu | image = [[File:Noso Hachimangu.jpg|260px|Noso Hachimangu]]
Haiden | location = 2-3 Miyamachi, Iizuka City, Fukuoka Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|33.6411|N|130.6875|E|region:JP-40|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ubl|Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Chūai|Empress Jingū|Takenouchi no Sukune|Heavenly and Earthly Deities}} | type = Former Prefectural Shrine; {{ill|Betsu Hyōsha|ja|別表神社}} | festivals = {{ubl|April 15|October 15}} | rituals = {{ubl|Doll Gratitude festival (人形感謝祭)|Nagoshinoharae (Chinowa Ritual)}} | map = {{location map|Japan Fukuoka|label=Noso Hachimangu|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''Noso Hachimangu''' (曩祖八幡宮) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} in {{ill|Iizuka City|ja|飯塚市}}, Fukuoka Prefecture, Japan. It is a former {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社}} and currently listed as a {{ill|Betsu Hyōsha|ja|別表神社}} under the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|ja|神社本庁}}. The shrine was previously known as "Noso-no-Miya" (納祖宮). The shrine is revered as the guardian deity of Iizuka and is also worshipped as a protector of children. == Deities Enshrined == The shrine venerates the following deities: * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} * {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}} * {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} * {{ill|Takenouchi no Sukune|ja|武内宿禰}} * {{ill|Heavenly and Earthly Deities|ja|天神地祇}} == History == According to legend, {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} established an altar in the "Noso Forest" on her return from the {{ill|Sankan Seibatsu|ja|三韓征伐}} campaign to offer prayers to the Heavenly and Earthly Deities and to bid farewell to her loyal retainers from Kyushu. The name "Iizuka" is said to originate from this occasion, as the people proclaimed, "We hope to see your face again someday," with "itsuka" (いつか, someday) evolving into "Iizuka." The shrine’s records suggest its foundation occurred soon after this event, though the exact date is unknown. By the {{ill|Enbun|ja|延文}} period (1359), shrine buildings had been constructed. It was designated as a Prefectural Shrine in {{ill|Taishō|ja|大正}} 13 (1924). == festivals and Rituals == * **Monthly festival** (Tsukinamisai): 1st day of every month * **Setsubun Star festival**: February 3 * **Doll Gratitude festival**: 2nd Sunday in May * **Nagoshinoharae (Chinowa Ritual)**: June 30 == Notes == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{Official website|name=Noso Hachimangu}} It is the main shrine of Iizuka in Fukuoka.{{Cite web |last=Ojisanjake |title=Noso Hachimangu |url=https://www.ojisanjake.com/2016/02/noso-hachimangu.html |access-date=2023-10-20 |language=en}} It is a [[Beppyo shrine]], or a shrine that is particularly notable in a certain way with a significant history to it.{{Cite web |title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー |url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/ |access-date=2023-04-02 |website=jinja-gosyuin.com}} It is a [[Hachiman shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to the [[kami]] [[Hachiman]]{{cite web |last=Motegi |first=Sadazumi |title=Shamei Bunpu (Shrine names and Distributions) |url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=776 |accessdate=23 March 2010 |publisher=Encyclopedia of Shinto |language=Japanese}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Noso Hachimangu | image = [[File:Noso Hachimangu.jpg|260px|Noso Hachimangu]]
Haiden | location = 2-3 Miyamachi, Iizuka City, Fukuoka Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|33.6411|N|130.6875|E|region:JP-40|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ubl|Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Chūai|Empress Jingū|Takenouchi no Sukune|Heavenly and Earthly Deities}} | type = Former Prefectural Shrine; {{ill|Betsu Hyōsha|ja|別表神社}} | festivals = {{ubl|April 15|October 15}} | rituals = {{ubl|Doll Gratitude festival (人形感謝祭)|Nagoshinoharae (Chinowa Ritual)}} | map = {{location map|Japan Fukuoka|label=Noso Hachimangu|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''Noso Hachimangu''' (曩祖八幡宮) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} in {{ill|Iizuka City|ja|飯塚市}}, Fukuoka Prefecture, Japan. It is a former {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社}} and currently listed as a {{ill|Betsu Hyōsha|ja|別表神社}} under the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|ja|神社本庁}}. The shrine was previously known as "Noso-no-Miya" (納祖宮). The shrine is revered as the guardian deity of Iizuka and is also worshipped as a protector of children. == Deities Enshrined == The shrine venerates the following deities: * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} * {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}} * {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} * {{ill|Takenouchi no Sukune|ja|武内宿禰}} * {{ill|Heavenly and Earthly Deities|ja|天神地祇}} == History == According to legend, {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} established an altar in the "Noso Forest" on her return from the {{ill|Sankan Seibatsu|ja|三韓征伐}} campaign to offer prayers to the Heavenly and Earthly Deities and to bid farewell to her loyal retainers from Kyushu. The name "Iizuka" is said to originate from this occasion, as the people proclaimed, "We hope to see your face again someday," with "itsuka" (いつか, someday) evolving into "Iizuka." The shrine’s records suggest its foundation occurred soon after this event, though the exact date is unknown. By the {{ill|Enbun|ja|延文}} period (1359), shrine buildings had been constructed. It was designated as a Prefectural Shrine in {{ill|Taishō|ja|大正}} 13 (1924). == festivals and Rituals == * **Monthly festival** (Tsukinamisai): 1st day of every month * **Setsubun Star festival**: February 3 * **Doll Gratitude festival**: 2nd Sunday in May * **Nagoshinoharae (Chinowa Ritual)**: June 30 == Notes == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{Official website|name=Noso Hachimangu}} Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, qq February 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox shinto shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = Noso Hachimangu.jpg | deity = [[Hachiman]] }} [[{{PAGENAME}}]] is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Japan]]. It is the main shrine of Iizuka in Fukuoka.{{Cite web |last=Ojisanjake |title=Noso Hachimangu |url=https://www.ojisanjake.com/2016/02/noso-hachimangu.html |access-date=2023-10-20 |language=en}} It is a [[Beppyo shrine]], or a shrine that is particularly notable in a certain way with a significant history to it.{{Cite web |title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー |url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/ |access-date=2023-04-02 |website=jinja-gosyuin.com}} It is a [[Hachiman shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to the [[kami]] [[Hachiman]]{{cite web |last=Motegi |first=Sadazumi |title=Shamei Bunpu (Shrine names and Distributions) |url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=776 |accessdate=23 March 2010 |publisher=Encyclopedia of Shinto |language=Japanese}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Noso Hachimangu | image = [[File:Noso Hachimangu.jpg|260px|Noso Hachimangu]]
Haiden | location = 2-3 Miyamachi, Iizuka City, Fukuoka Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|33.64qq|N|130.6875|E|region:JP-40|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ubl|Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Chūai|Empress Jingū|Takenouchi no Sukune|Heavenly and Earthly Deities}} | type = Former Prefectural Shrine; {{ill|Betsu Hyōsha|ja|別表神社}} | festivals = {{ubl|April 15|October 15}} | rituals = {{ubl|Doll Gratitude festival (人形感謝祭)|Nagoshinoharae (Chinowa Ritual)}} | map = {{location map|Japan Fukuoka|label=Noso Hachimangu|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''Noso Hachimangu''' (曩祖八幡宮) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} in {{ill|Iizuka City|ja|飯塚市}}, Fukuoka Prefecture, Japan. It is a former {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社}} and currently listed as a {{ill|Betsu Hyōsha|ja|別表神社}} under the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|ja|神社本庁}}. The shrine was previously known as "Noso-no-Miya" (納祖宮). The shrine is revered as the guardian deity of Iizuka and is also worshipped as a protector of children. == Deities Enshrined == The shrine venerates the following deities: * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} * {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}} * {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} * {{ill|Takenouchi no Sukune|ja|武内宿禰}} * {{ill|Heavenly and Earthly Deities|ja|天神地祇}} == History == According to legend, {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} established an altar in the "Noso Forest" on her return from the {{ill|Sankan Seibatsu|ja|三韓征伐}} campaign to offer prayers to the Heavenly and Earthly Deities and to bid farewell to her loyal retainers from Kyushu. The name "Iizuka" is said to originate from this occasion, as the people proclaimed, "We hope to see your face again someday," with "itsuka" (いつか, someday) evolving into "Iizuka." The shrine’s records suggest its foundation occurred soon after this event, though the exact date is unknown. By the {{ill|Enbun|ja|延文}} period (1359), shrine buildings had been constructed. It was designated as a Prefectural Shrine in {{ill|Taishō|ja|大正}} 13 (1924). == festivals and Rituals == * **Monthly festival** (Tsukinamisai): 1st day of every month * **Setsubun Star festival**: February 3 * **Doll Gratitude festival**: 2nd Sunday in May * **Nagoshinoharae (Chinowa Ritual)**: June 30 == Notes == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{Official website|name=Noso Hachimangu}} Haiden | location = 2-3 Miyamachi, Iizuka City, Fukuoka Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|33.64qq|N|130.6875|E|region:JP-40|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ubl|Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Chūai|Empress Jingū|Takenouchi no Sukune|Heavenly and Earthly Deities}} | type = Former Prefectural Shrine; {{ill|Betsu Hyōsha|ja|別表神社}} | festivals = {{ubl|April 15|October 15}} | rituals = {{ubl|Doll Gratitude festival (人形感謝祭)|Nagoshinoharae (Chinowa Ritual)}} | map = {{location map|Japan Fukuoka|label=Noso Hachimangu|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''Noso Hachimangu''' (曩祖八幡宮) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} in {{ill|Iizuka City|ja|飯塚市}}, Fukuoka Prefecture, Japan. It is a former {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社}} and currently listed as a {{ill|Betsu Hyōsha|ja|別表神社}} under the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|ja|神社本庁}}. The shrine was previously known as "Noso-no-Miya" (納祖宮). The shrine is revered as the guardian deity of Iizuka and is also worshipped as a protector of children. == Deities Enshrined == The shrine venerates the following deities: * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} * {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}} * {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} * {{ill|Takenouchi no Sukune|ja|武内宿禰}} * {{ill|Heavenly and Earthly Deities|ja|天神地祇}} == History == According to legend, {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} established an altar in the "Noso Forest" on her return from the {{ill|Sankan Seibatsu|ja|三韓征伐}} campaign to offer prayers to the Heavenly and Earthly Deities and to bid farewell to her loyal retainers from Kyushu. The name "Iizuka" is said to originate from this occasion, as the people proclaimed, "We hope to see your face again someday," with "itsuka" (いつか, someday) evolving into "Iizuka." The shrine’s records suggest its foundation occurred soon after this event, though the exact date is unknown. By the {{ill|Enbun|ja|延文}} period (1359), shrine buildings had been constructed. It was designated as a Prefectural Shrine in {{ill|Taishō|ja|大正}} 13 (1924). == festivals and Rituals == * **Monthly festival** (Tsukinamisai): 1st day of every month * **Setsubun Star festival**: February 3 * **Doll Gratitude festival**: 2nd Sunday in May * **Nagoshinoharae (Chinowa Ritual)**: June 30 == Notes == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{Official website|name=Noso Hachimangu}} [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, qq February 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox shinto shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = Noso Hachimangu.jpg | deity = [[Hachiman]] }} [[{{PAGENAME}}]] is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Japan]]. It is the main shrine of Iizuka in Fukuoka.{{Cite web |last=Ojisanjake |title=Noso Hachimangu |url=https://www.ojisanjake.com/2016/02/noso-hachimangu.html |access-date=2023-10-20 |language=en}} It is a [[Beppyo shrine]], or a shrine that is particularly notable in a certain way with a significant history to it.{{Cite web |title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー |url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/ |access-date=2023-04-02 |website=jinja-gosyuin.com}} It is a [[Hachiman shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to the [[kami]] [[Hachiman]]{{cite web |last=Motegi |first=Sadazumi |title=Shamei Bunpu (Shrine names and Distributions) |url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=776 |accessdate=23 March 2010 |publisher=Encyclopedia of Shinto |language=Japanese}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Noso Hachimangu | image = [[File:Noso Hachimangu.jpg|260px|Noso Hachimangu]]
Haiden | location = 2-3 Miyamachi, Iizuka City, Fukuoka Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|33.64qq|N|130.6875|E|region:JP-40|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ubl|Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Chūai|Empress Jingū|Takenouchi no Sukune|Heavenly and Earthly Deities}} | type = Former Prefectural Shrine; {{ill|Betsu Hyōsha|ja|別表神社}} | festivals = {{ubl|April 15|October 15}} | rituals = {{ubl|Doll Gratitude festival (人形感謝祭)|Nagoshinoharae (Chinowa Ritual)}} | map = {{location map|Japan Fukuoka|label=Noso Hachimangu|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''Noso Hachimangu''' (曩祖八幡宮) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} in {{ill|Iizuka City|ja|飯塚市}}, Fukuoka Prefecture, Japan. It is a former {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社}} and currently listed as a {{ill|Betsu Hyōsha|ja|別表神社}} under the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|ja|神社本庁}}. The shrine was previously known as "Noso-no-Miya" (納祖宮). The shrine is revered as the guardian deity of Iizuka and is also worshipped as a protector of children. == Deities Enshrined == The shrine venerates the following deities: * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} * {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}} * {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} * {{ill|Takenouchi no Sukune|ja|武内宿禰}} * {{ill|Heavenly and Earthly Deities|ja|天神地祇}} == History == According to legend, {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} established an altar in the "Noso Forest" on her return from the {{ill|Sankan Seibatsu|ja|三韓征伐}} campaign to offer prayers to the Heavenly and Earthly Deities and to bid farewell to her loyal retainers from Kyushu. The name "Iizuka" is said to originate from this occasion, as the people proclaimed, "We hope to see your face again someday," with "itsuka" (いつか, someday) evolving into "Iizuka." The shrine’s records suggest its foundation occurred soon after this event, though the exact date is unknown. By the {{ill|Enbun|ja|延文}} period (1359), shrine buildings had been constructed. It was designated as a Prefectural Shrine in {{ill|Taishō|ja|大正}} 13 (1924). == festivals and Rituals == * **Monthly festival** (Tsukinamisai): 1st day of every month * **Setsubun Star festival**: February 3 * **Doll Gratitude festival**: 2nd Sunday in May * **Nagoshinoharae (Chinowa Ritual)**: June 30 == Notes == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{Official website|name=Noso Hachimangu}} Haiden | location = 2-3 Miyamachi, Iizuka City, Fukuoka Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|33.64qq|N|130.6875|E|region:JP-40|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ubl|Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Chūai|Empress Jingū|Takenouchi no Sukune|Heavenly and Earthly Deities}} | type = Former Prefectural Shrine; {{ill|Betsu Hyōsha|ja|別表神社}} | festivals = {{ubl|April 15|October 15}} | rituals = {{ubl|Doll Gratitude festival (人形感謝祭)|Nagoshinoharae (Chinowa Ritual)}} | map = {{location map|Japan Fukuoka|label=Noso Hachimangu|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''Noso Hachimangu''' (曩祖八幡宮) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} in {{ill|Iizuka City|ja|飯塚市}}, Fukuoka Prefecture, Japan. It is a former {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社}} and currently listed as a {{ill|Betsu Hyōsha|ja|別表神社}} under the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|ja|神社本庁}}. The shrine was previously known as "Noso-no-Miya" (納祖宮). The shrine is revered as the guardian deity of Iizuka and is also worshipped as a protector of children. == Deities Enshrined == The shrine venerates the following deities: * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} * {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}} * {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} * {{ill|Takenouchi no Sukune|ja|武内宿禰}} * {{ill|Heavenly and Earthly Deities|ja|天神地祇}} == History == According to legend, {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} established an altar in the "Noso Forest" on her return from the {{ill|Sankan Seibatsu|ja|三韓征伐}} campaign to offer prayers to the Heavenly and Earthly Deities and to bid farewell to her loyal retainers from Kyushu. The name "Iizuka" is said to originate from this occasion, as the people proclaimed, "We hope to see your face again someday," with "itsuka" (いつか, someday) evolving into "Iizuka." The shrine’s records suggest its foundation occurred soon after this event, though the exact date is unknown. By the {{ill|Enbun|ja|延文}} period (1359), shrine buildings had been constructed. It was designated as a Prefectural Shrine in {{ill|Taishō|ja|大正}} 13 (1924). == festivals and Rituals == * **Monthly festival** (Tsukinamisai): 1st day of every month * **Setsubun Star festival**: February 3 * **Doll Gratitude festival**: 2nd Sunday in May * **Nagoshinoharae (Chinowa Ritual)**: June 30 == Notes == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{Official website|name=Noso Hachimangu}} Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, qq February 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox shinto shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = Noso Hachimangu.jpg | deity = [[Hachiman]] }} [[{{PAGENAME}}]] is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Japan]]. It is the main shrine of Iizuka in Fukuoka.{{Cite web |last=Ojisanjake |title=Noso Hachimangu |url=https://www.ojisanjake.com/2016/02/noso-hachimangu.html |access-date=2023-10-20 |language=en}} It is a [[Beppyo shrine]], or a shrine that is particularly notable in a certain way with a significant history to it.{{Cite web |title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー |url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/ |access-date=2023-04-02 |website=jinja-gosyuin.com}} It is a [[Hachiman shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to the [[kami]] [[Hachiman]]{{cite web |last=Motegi |first=Sadazumi |title=Shamei Bunpu (Shrine names and Distributions) |url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=776 |accessdate=23 March 2010 |publisher=Encyclopedia of Shinto |language=Japanese}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Noso Hachimangu | image = [[File:Noso Hachimangu.jpg|260px|Noso Hachimangu]]
Haiden | location = 2-3 Miyamachi, Iizuka City, Fukuoka Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|33.64qq|N|130.6875|E|region:JP-40|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ubl|Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Chūai|Empress Jingū|Takenouchi no Sukune|Heavenly and Earthly Deities}} | type = Former Prefectural Shrine; {{ill|Betsu Hyōsha|ja|別表神社}} | festivals = {{ubl|April 15|October 15}} | rituals = {{ubl|Doll Gratitude festival (人形感謝祭)|Nagoshinoharae (Chinowa Ritual)}} | map = {{location map|Japan Fukuoka|label=Noso Hachimangu|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''Noso Hachimangu''' (曩祖八幡宮) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} in {{ill|Iizuka City|ja|飯塚市}}, Fukuoka Prefecture, Japan. It is a former {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社}} and currently listed as a {{ill|Betsu Hyōsha|ja|別表神社}} under the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|ja|神社本庁}}. The shrine was previously known as "Noso-no-Miya" (納祖宮). The shrine is revered as the guardian deity of Iizuka and is also worshipped as a protector of children. == Deities Enshrined == The shrine venerates the following deities: * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} * {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}} * {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} * {{ill|Takenouchi no Sukune|ja|武内宿禰}} * {{ill|Heavenly and Earthly Deities|ja|天神地祇}} == History == According to legend, {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} established an altar in the "Noso Forest" on her return from the {{ill|Sankan Seibatsu|ja|三韓征伐}} campaign to offer prayers to the Heavenly and Earthly Deities and to bid farewell to her loyal retainers from Kyushu. The name "Iizuka" is said to originate from this occasion, as the people proclaimed, "We hope to see your face again someday," with "itsuka" (いつか, someday) evolving into "Iizuka." The shrine’s records suggest its foundation occurred soon after this event, though the exact date is unknown. By the {{ill|Enbun|ja|延文}} period (1359), shrine buildings had been constructed. It was designated as a Prefectural Shrine in {{ill|Taishō|ja|大正}} 13 (1924). == festivals and Rituals == * **Monthly festival** (Tsukinamisai): 1st day of every month * **Setsubun Star festival**: February 3 * **Doll Gratitude festival**: 2nd Sunday in May * **Nagoshinoharae (Chinowa Ritual)**: June 30 == Notes == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{Official website|name=Noso Hachimangu}} Haiden | location = 2-3 Miyamachi, Iizuka City, Fukuoka Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|33.6411|N|130.6875|E|region:JP-40|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ubl|Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Chūai|Empress Jingū|Takenouchi no Sukune|Heavenly and Earthly Deities}} | type = Former Prefectural Shrine; {{ill|Betsu Hyōsha|ja|別表神社}} | festivals = {{ubl|April 15|October 15}} | rituals = {{ubl|Doll Gratitude festival (人形感謝祭)|Nagoshinoharae (Chinowa Ritual)}} | map = {{location map|Japan Fukuoka|label=Noso Hachimangu|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''Noso Hachimangu''' (曩祖八幡宮) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} in {{ill|Iizuka City|ja|飯塚市}}, Fukuoka Prefecture, Japan. It is a former {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社}} and currently listed as a {{ill|Betsu Hyōsha|ja|別表神社}} under the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|ja|神社本庁}}. The shrine was previously known as "Noso-no-Miya" (納祖宮). The shrine is revered as the guardian deity of Iizuka and is also worshipped as a protector of children. == Deities Enshrined == The shrine venerates the following deities: * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} * {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}} * {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} * {{ill|Takenouchi no Sukune|ja|武内宿禰}} * {{ill|Heavenly and Earthly Deities|ja|天神地祇}} == History == According to legend, {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} established an altar in the "Noso Forest" on her return from the {{ill|Sankan Seibatsu|ja|三韓征伐}} campaign to offer prayers to the Heavenly and Earthly Deities and to bid farewell to her loyal retainers from Kyushu. The name "Iizuka" is said to originate from this occasion, as the people proclaimed, "We hope to see your face again someday," with "itsuka" (いつか, someday) evolving into "Iizuka." The shrine’s records suggest its foundation occurred soon after this event, though the exact date is unknown. By the {{ill|Enbun|ja|延文}} period (1359), shrine buildings had been constructed. It was designated as a Prefectural Shrine in {{ill|Taishō|ja|大正}} 13 (1924). == festivals and Rituals == * **Monthly festival** (Tsukinamisai): 1st day of every month * **Setsubun Star festival**: February 3 * **Doll Gratitude festival**: 2nd Sunday in May * **Nagoshinoharae (Chinowa Ritual)**: June 30 == Notes == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{Official website|name=Noso Hachimangu}}
